vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1787: Provider default harnesses, role-level harness overrides, and real brand logos

plan.title: Provider default harnesses, role-level harness overrides, and real brand logos
plan.author: agent:claude-fable-5

references.label: PRD rev 3 (primary execution reference — every bead cites its WI-N section)

narratives.Problem: Every unset harness silently falls back to claude-code even for OpenAI/Google/Kimi models; harness resolution logic is duplicated inline at five spawn sites; role-level harness overrides exist in schema but only Flywheel has UI; legacy specialist_harnesses (PAN-636) is an undocumented parallel config; live model/harness switching has no fresh-session guarantee on the JSONL --resume path and only a 30s grace; only 2 of 10 providers have real brand marks and pi agents render without a brand mark.
narratives.Proposal: Implement the PRD at .pan/drafts/PAN-1787.md (rev 3), which carries verified file/line references and before/after snippets for every change: (1) built-in provider→harness defaults on the PROVIDERS registry (anthropic→claude-code, openai→codex, all others→pi) with getBuiltInDefaultHarness(); (2) a single exported resolveHarness() (explicit > role > provider override > built-in default > claude-code, always ending at the canUseHarnessSync ToS gate, collapsing to claude-code when the winning binary is missing) migrated into all five spawn sites; (3) specialist_harnesses reconciled as a deprecated role-tier alias; (4) session-safe switching: 60s graceful grace + Escape-interrupt for tmux-TUI harnesses, plus a fresh-session invariant that drops JSONL --resume across any model/harness change; (5) conversation harness switching kept but labeled experimental; (6) Settings UI: honest Default(<built-in>) provider selects with key-deletion on clear, and a harness override select on every role card; (7) a shared branding registry (PROVIDER_BRANDS + HARNESS_BRANDS, real SVG marks for 10 providers + 3 harnesses, π for pi) rolled out across composer picker, Settings, shared ModelPicker, agent dock, and conversation feed, with the dead Settings/Provider/ directory deleted. Line-drift corrections verified 2026-06-12: plan-kickoff chain now at spawn-planning-session.ts:558-561; CLI --harness flags at cli/index.ts:376/515/533/536; convertConversationTranscript exported at session-format-converter.ts:304.
narratives.NonGoals:
- Sub-role harness overrides (RoleSubConfig keeps only model)
- New harnesses beyond claude-code / pi / codex
- Per-project harness configuration in projects.yaml
- Any change to conversation model-switch tiers 1–3 (switch-strategy.ts behavior byte-for-byte unchanged)
- Any change to the transcript converter, the tier-4 endpoint path (routes/conversations.ts:2944-2971), or their tests — label-only work
- Modifying or weakening canUseHarnessSync (harness-policy.ts)
- The approval-gated planning handoff and planning-flow inconsistency fixes (moved to PAN-1788 WI-22; only the stale --harness help strings stay here)
- Transcript conversion/migration for agents (fresh session + continue.json re-onboarding is the only agent switching mechanism)

autoDecisions.summary: Decomposed the PRD's 10 work items into 24 beads; PRD phases are guidance, not bead boundaries.
autoDecisions.rationale: Planning template requires aggressively small, independently reviewable/mergeable beads; each WI splits cleanly along file/surface lines.

autoDecisions.summary: The built-in-default activity-log notice is emitted inside resolveHarness() itself, once per provider per process; no separate release-notes work item.
autoDecisions.rationale: resolveHarness is the single choke point that knows which tier won; release notes are generated from git log by pan release, so the commit message carries the behavior-change callout.

autoDecisions.summary: Harness binary availability check is a new async helper inside harness-resolve.ts (promisified exec `command -v <bin>`, module-level cache); doctor's checkCommand is NOT reused.
autoDecisions.rationale: checkCommand in src/cli/commands/doctor.ts uses execSync, which is forbidden in dashboard-server-reachable code (PAN-70 repo rule); resolveHarness runs server-side.

autoDecisions.summary: Pi gets no pre-warning interrupt in graceful restart — 60s grace only.
autoDecisions.rationale: Verified 2026-06-12: pi-fifo.ts exposes no interrupt/abort/cancel op on the rpc.in FIFO; the PRD names this exact fallback. Codex/claude-code get the tmux Escape path.

autoDecisions.summary: Resume guard backward compat: JSONL sessions lacking persisted origin model/harness metadata keep resuming as today; the guard applies to sessions created after the change.
autoDecisions.rationale: Pre-change sessions cannot be distinguished safely; deacon auto-resume never changes model/harness, and restartAgent already always spawns fresh, so the residual risk window is empty.

autoDecisions.summary: Dead-code deletion widened from ProviderCard.tsx to the entire components/Settings/Provider/ directory (ProviderCard, ProviderPanel, ThinkingLevelSlider).
autoDecisions.rationale: Verified 2026-06-12 that ProviderCard's only importer is ProviderPanel, and ProviderPanel/ThinkingLevelSlider have zero importers in live code (one comment mention in a test). PRD's claim that ProviderCard was unimported was stale by one hop.

autoDecisions.summary: Dashboard start route keeps its body shape-validation and fail-fast policy check; it forwards --harness to pan start only when the user picked one, and passes nothing otherwise.
autoDecisions.rationale: Matches PRD WI-2 ('keep body validation; pass validated value as explicit'); avoids converting 'unset' into an explicit tier, which would double-fire the built-in-default notice and bypass role overrides applied at spawn time.

autoDecisions.summary: PRD line-number drifts corrected in bead text rather than halting: plan-kickoff chain at spawn-planning-session.ts:558-561 (PRD said 513-516); CLI --harness at cli/index.ts:376/515/533/536 (PRD said 374/512/530/533); convertConversationTranscript export at session-format-converter.ts:304.
autoDecisions.rationale: PRD itself instructs 'if a line number has drifted, search for the quoted code'; quoted code verified present 2026-06-12.

autoDecisions.summary: AgentIcon's current pi rendering is the lucide Bot fallback (providerForAgent returns null), not the Anthropic starburst the PRD describes; the fix is identical (harness→HarnessLogo with π).
autoDecisions.rationale: Verified AgentIcon.tsx:14-35 on 2026-06-12; the acceptance criterion 'pi never renders the Anthropic starburst' remains valid and gains 'nor the generic Bot glyph for known harnesses'.

items.title: Built-in defaultHarness on the provider registry

narrative.Action: Per PRD WI-1: in src/lib/providers.ts add `defaultHarness: RuntimeName` to the ProviderConfig interface (line 29; import RuntimeName from './runtimes/types.js'), set it on all 10 PROVIDERS entries (lines 61-163: anthropic→'claude-code', openai→'codex', google/kimi/minimax/zai/mimo/openrouter/nous/dashscope→'pi'), and export `getBuiltInDefaultHarness(provider: ProviderName): RuntimeName` returning the registry value with 'claude-code' for unknown providers. Add unit coverage in tests/unit/lib/providers.test.ts (create if absent, mirroring tests/unit/lib/ layout).

items.title: Given the PROVIDERS registry, every entry stores a defaultHarness (anthropic→claude-code, openai→codex, the other eight→pi) and typecheck fails if an entry omits the now-required field
getBuiltInDefaultHarness('openai') returns 'codex', ('anthropic') returns 'claude-code', ('kimi') returns 'pi', and an unknown provider id returns 'claude-code'
A unit test in tests/unit/lib/providers.test.ts validates the three-way mapping for all 10 providers and the unknown-provider claude-code fallback
Unified resolveHarness() in new src/lib/harness-resolve.ts

narrative.Action: Per PRD WI-2: create src/lib/harness-resolve.ts exporting `resolveHarness(input: { explicit?: RuntimeName; role?: Role; model: string }): Promise<RuntimeName>` with precedence (first defined wins): 1) explicit, 2) loadYamlConfig().config.roles[role]?.harness, 3) config.providerHarnesses[provider] where provider = getProviderForModelSync(model).name, 4) getBuiltInDefaultHarness(provider), 5) 'claude-code'. The winner ALWAYS passes through canUseHarnessSync(winner, model, await getProviderAuthMode(model)); blocked ⇒ 'claude-code'. If the winner is 'pi' or 'codex' and its binary is not installed, collapse to 'claude-code' with a console warning — implement an async availability helper using promisified exec `command -v <bin>` with a module-level cache (do NOT reuse doctor's execSync-based checkCommand; resolveHarness is dashboard-server-reachable and execSync is forbidden there). When tier 4 (built-in default) decides, emit a one-line notice once per provider per process: "harness <h> chosen by provider default — override in Settings → Providers". Write tests/unit/lib/harness-resolve.test.ts covering the full precedence matrix: explicit>role>provider-override>built-in; empty config + anthropic⇒claude-code, + openai⇒codex, + kimi/google/zai⇒pi; policy-blocked winner (explicit pi + anthropic model + subscription auth) collapses to claude-code; missing binary collapses to claude-code with warning. Mock the availability check and policy/auth lookups in tests.

items.title: resolveHarness returns the explicit harness when provided, else the role override, else the provider override, else the built-in default — the full precedence-matrix unit suite passes
With empty providerHarnesses and no role override, resolveHarness returns claude-code for an Anthropic model, codex for an OpenAI model, and pi for kimi/google/zai models
Every winner passes through canUseHarnessSync: explicit pi + Anthropic model + subscription auth returns claude-code; existing harness-policy tests pass unmodified
When the winning pi/codex binary is absent, resolveHarness returns claude-code and logs a warning, using only async exec (no execSync anywhere in the new module)
The first resolution that lands on a built-in default emits the one-line override-hint notice exactly once per provider per process
Migrate agents.ts spawn sites to resolveHarness()

narrative.Action: Per PRD WI-2 migration table, in src/lib/agents.ts replace the inline chains with one resolveHarness() call each: spawnRun (lines ~3011-3013: `options.harness ?? loadYamlConfig().config.roles?.[role]?.harness` then resolveEffectiveHarness) becomes `await resolveHarness({ explicit: options.harness, role, model: selectedModel })`; spawnAgent (~3339-3341) same pattern; restartAgent (~4736-4738) becomes `await resolveHarness({ explicit: newHarness ?? agentState.harness, role: agentState.role, model: effectiveModel })`. Delete resolveEffectiveHarness (lines 329-336) or reduce it to a one-line deprecated wrapper over resolveHarness if external callers exist (grep first). Keep getProviderAuthMode exported — resolveHarness uses it. Behavior assertions: with empty config, spawnAgent persists harness 'codex' for an openai model and 'pi' for a kimi model in agent state.

items.title: spawnRun, spawnAgent, and restartAgent each call resolveHarness() exactly once; the diff deletes the inline `options.harness ?? roles[role].harness` chains from agents.ts
The diff deletes resolveEffectiveHarness or reduces it to a thin wrapper that returns resolveHarness's result
With empty harness config, a work-agent spawn with an OpenAI model persists harness 'codex' and a kimi model persists 'pi' in agent state (unit test)
npm run typecheck exits 0 and the existing harness-policy suite runs green with zero modifications to its test files
Migrate planning kickoff to resolveHarness()

narrative.Action: In src/lib/planning/spawn-planning-session.ts replace the inline chain at lines 558-561 (NOTE: PRD says 513-516 — it drifted; search for `const requestedHarness = opts.harness ?? config.roles?.plan?.harness ?? providerDefaultHarness ?? 'claude-code'`) with `const effectiveHarness = await resolveHarness({ explicit: opts.harness, role: 'plan', model: planningModel })`, deleting the local providerDefaultHarness lookup (line 558) and the manual canUseHarnessSync call (lines 560-561). Keep the log line reporting the final model+harness. Remove now-unused imports (canUseHarnessSync, getProviderAuthMode if unused elsewhere in the file).

items.title: spawn-planning-session.ts resolves the kickoff harness through one resolveHarness() call; the diff deletes the inline precedence chain and the direct canUseHarnessSync call
Planning kickoff with empty harness config and an OpenAI planning model resolves harness 'codex'; explicit opts.harness still wins (unit test)
npm run typecheck exits 0 and lint shows no unused-import errors in spawn-planning-session.ts
Dashboard start route: validate, fail fast, forward explicit harness only

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/server/routes/agents.ts (lines ~3156-3165): keep the body.harness union shape-validation exactly as-is; keep a fail-fast policy check for a user-picked harness (may call resolveHarness with the pick as explicit, or keep the existing canUseHarnessSync call — either is acceptable as long as the user gets a collapsed-to-claude-code result before spawn). The route must forward `--harness <value>` to the `pan start` invocation at ~:3435 ONLY when the user explicitly picked one; when body.harness is unset, pass no --harness flag so the spawned `pan start` resolves via the full chain (role → provider override → built-in). Do NOT resolve-and-forward an unset harness as if it were explicit — that would bypass role overrides and double-fire the built-in-default notice.

items.title: POST start with body.harness='pi' on a policy-blocked combo spawns with claude-code (fail-fast collapse preserved); invalid harness strings are ignored exactly as today
When body.harness is unset, the spawned pan start command contains no --harness flag (assert on spawn args in a unit/route test)
When body.harness is set and allowed, the spawn args contain --harness with that exact value
Fix stale --harness help strings + widen strike harness type + sync wrapper skills

narrative.Action: In src/cli/index.ts update the three stale --harness help strings that still read 'claude-code (default) | pi' to 'Coding-agent harness: claude-code | pi | codex (defaults to role/provider settings)'. Verified locations (PRD's 374/512/530 drifted): line 376 (pan plan), line 515 (pan start), line 533 (pan strike). At line 536 widen the strike action's option type from `harness?: 'claude-code' | 'pi'` to `harness?: RuntimeName` (import type from lib/runtimes/types.js). Then update the wrapper skills that quote these flags (skills/pan-plan/SKILL.md, skills/pan-start/SKILL.md, and any other SKILL.md mentioning --harness — grep skills/ for 'harness') in the SAME commit; scripts/lint-skills.sh (runs in npm run lint) must exit 0.

items.title: Each of pan plan --help, pan start --help, and pan strike --help prints the --harness description listing claude-code | pi | codex with the role/provider-settings default wording
Given --harness codex, the strike action accepts it without a type error (typecheck exits 0 with the widened RuntimeName type)
npm run lint exits 0, including scripts/lint-skills.sh cross-checking the updated SKILL.md files against the new --help output
Reconcile legacy specialist_harnesses as deprecated role-tier alias

narrative.Action: Per PRD WI-3: model_selection.specialist_harnesses (src/lib/cloister/config.ts:157-163, defaults at :347; read by getSpecialistHarness in src/lib/cloister/router.ts:94-98) must not survive as an undocumented fourth resolution tier. Make it a deprecated alias read at the SAME precedence as roles[<role>].harness (the role tier of resolveHarness): when both roles[role].harness and the matching specialist_harnesses key are set, roles wins; when only the legacy key is set, it applies at role tier; log a one-time deprecation warning naming the legacy key and the replacement (roles.<role>.harness) when the legacy value is the one that applied. Implement by having the role-tier lookup used for specialist spawns consult roles[role].harness first, then the alias via getSpecialistHarness. Update src/lib/cloister/__tests__/specialist-harnesses-config.test.ts and specialist-harness-router.test.ts to the alias semantics (do not delete coverage — rewrite assertions).

items.title: With only specialist_harnesses.<x> set, a specialist spawn resolves to that harness (alias applies at role tier)
When both roles.<role>.harness and the matching specialist_harnesses key are set to different values, resolution returns the roles value
The first time the legacy alias decides a harness, the process logs a deprecation warning naming roles.<role>.harness exactly once
The updated specialist-harnesses-config suite validates alias-applies semantics and the updated specialist-harness-router suite validates roles-wins precedence; both run green
sendEscapeKeyAsync tmux helper

metadata.acJustification: Single small helper; two criteria cover behavior and repo-rule compliance.

narrative.Action: Per PRD WI-4 step 2: add `export async function sendEscapeKeyAsync(sessionName: string, times = 1): Promise<void>` to src/lib/tmux.ts. For each press run tmux send-keys targeting the exact pane (use the existing exactPaneTarget/exactSession helpers at tmux.ts:295-316 to avoid prefix-matching the wrong session) with the literal key name `Escape`, then wait 250ms between presses via `await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 250))`. Async-only (repo rule: never add sync tmux callers); use the same async exec pattern as neighbouring async helpers (e.g. isHarnessProcessAlive at :509). Unit test with vi.useFakeTimers(): assert N invocations of the tmux command with Escape and the 250ms gaps advanced via advanceTimersByTimeAsync — never real waits.

items.title: sendEscapeKeyAsync(session, 2) sends exactly two tmux send-keys Escape commands to the exact-match pane target with a 250ms gap (fake-timer unit test)
The helper contains no execSync/spawnSync and its tests use fake timers only (no real waits)
Graceful restart: 60s grace + Escape interrupt for TUI harnesses

narrative.Action: Per PRD WI-4 in src/lib/agents.ts restartAgent graceful branch (lines ~4715-4732): extract `const GRACEFUL_RESTART_GRACE_MS = 60_000;` at module level, change the warning text to say 60s, and replace both the hardcoded 30s string and the `setTimeout(r, 30_000)` wait. Keep the stale-continue.json mtime check unchanged. BEFORE sending the warning: when agentState.harness is 'claude-code' or 'codex', call `await sendEscapeKeyAsync(normalizedId, 2)` (two presses, 250ms apart) to interrupt in-flight tool calls, wait ~1s, then deliver the warning via the existing sendKeys path. For harness 'pi' skip the interrupt entirely (verified: pi-fifo.ts has no interrupt op; 60s grace still applies). Non-graceful path unchanged. IMPLEMENTATION CHECKPOINT (PRD WI-4 step 3): in a live claude-code session, Esc during streaming interrupts but a second Esc on an idle prompt can open the history-rewind dialog — verify with `tmux -L panopticon capture-pane -t <session> -p` that the warning text still lands after double-Esc; if the rewind dialog swallows it, drop to a single Esc and record the decision in the PR description and continue.json. Tests: fake timers only (vi.useFakeTimers + advanceTimersByTimeAsync(60_000)); assert warning contains '60s', escape helper called twice for claude-code and codex, zero times for pi.

items.title: Graceful restart waits GRACEFUL_RESTART_GRACE_MS (60000) and sends a warning whose text shows '60s' (fake-timer test; no real waits)
For claude-code and codex agents the graceful flow sends Escape twice before the warning; for pi agents it sends no Escape (unit tests per harness)
Non-graceful restart sends no Escape and performs no grace wait (existing behavior preserved by test)
Live checkpoint executed: capture-pane shows the 60s warning visible after the double-Esc, or the implementation drops to single Esc with the decision recorded in continue.json and the PR description
Persist session-origin model+harness beside claudeSessionId

narrative.Action: Per PRD WI-5 step 1: extend the agent runtime-state shape where claudeSessionId lives (src/lib/agents.ts:2037 type field, :2069 snapshot read, :2159-2167 patch write) with `sessionModel?: string` and `sessionHarness?: RuntimeName`, and populate them wherever a NEW JSONL session id is recorded (the write at ~:3127 where rawSessionId is stored, plus any other claudeSessionId-setting path found by grepping `claudeSessionId:` in agents.ts). The fields record the model and harness the session was CREATED with and are never rewritten on resume. Unit test: after a spawn, runtime state contains sessionModel/sessionHarness matching the spawn's resolved values; after a resume of the same session they are unchanged.

items.title: Creating a new agent session persists sessionModel and sessionHarness alongside claudeSessionId in runtime state (unit test reads them back)
When an existing session resumes, runtime state shows the original sessionModel/sessionHarness unchanged (test asserts no rewrite on resume)
Loading runtime state written before this change (no origin fields) returns the snapshot without error (backward-compat test)
Fresh-session invariant: drop --resume across model/harness change

narrative.Action: Per PRD WI-5 steps 2-3, decision rule: a model change, a harness change, or both ⇒ fresh harness session; JSONL --resume is only used when both are unchanged; no transcript conversion for agents, ever. In every path that sets spawnMode:'resume' — buildAgentLaunchConfig consumer at src/lib/agents.ts:2647-2684 and resumeAgent (~:4537, the `...(compactSeed ? {} : { spawnMode: 'resume', resumeSessionId: sessionId })` spread) — compare the agent's current state.model/state.harness against the persisted sessionModel/sessionHarness from the session-origin-state bead. On mismatch: drop resumeSessionId/spawnMode (fresh session), use the existing continue.json re-onboarding prompt (the restartAgent prompt at ~:4787 is the model), and log one line stating which field drifted. Sessions with NO persisted origin metadata (pre-change) resume as today (recorded autoDecision). restartAgent already never passes resumeSessionId — leave it; add a regression test locking that. Tests: restart with newModel only / newHarness only / both ⇒ generated launcher contains no --resume; resume with unchanged model+harness keeps --resume <id>; resume with drifted model drops it; resume with missing origin metadata keeps --resume.

items.title: A resume attempt whose current model or harness differs from the persisted session origin produces a launcher with no --resume and logs which field drifted
A resume with unchanged model+harness still emits --resume <sessionId> in the launcher (status quo locked by test)
restartAgent with newModel only, newHarness only, or both creates a launcher containing no --resume (regression tests)
Given a session lacking origin metadata, the resume path emits --resume unchanged (backward-compat test)
Label live-conversation harness switching experimental

narrative.Action: Per PRD WI-6 — surface-level only, NO endpoint/converter/test changes. In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/chat/ModelPicker.tsx harness rows: when the picker operates on a LIVE conversation (thread a `liveConversation` boolean prop from ChatPanel if the component cannot already distinguish the new-conversation composer) and the row's harness differs from the current one, render a small muted 'Experimental' text tag (pan-style-guide: muted text, NO pill colors). Add tooltip/description copy for live-conversation harness rows: 'Experimental: converts the transcript to the new harness format. May lose fidelity; falls back to a fresh session on failure.' Docs: add a 'Switching harness on a live conversation (experimental)' subsection to configuration/harnesses.mdx stating unsupported/best-effort converter/fresh-session fallback/report issues but no support promise. The tier-4 path (routes/conversations.ts:2944-2971), decideSwitchStrategy, session-format-converter.ts (export now at :304), and all their tests stay byte-for-byte unchanged.

items.title: In a live conversation, harness rows other than the current harness render a muted Experimental tag; the new-conversation composer renders no tag (component test)
Live-conversation harness rows expose the transcript-conversion warning copy (tooltip/description renders the 'May lose fidelity' text)
configuration/harnesses.mdx shows the new 'Switching harness on a live conversation (experimental)' subsection stating the fresh-session fallback and best-effort status
git diff shows zero changes under routes/conversations.ts, switch-strategy.ts, session-format-converter.ts, and their test files
Settings API: expose built-in defaults + delete cleared provider harness keys

narrative.Action: Per PRD WI-7 backend half: (1) extend the settings GET payload (src/lib/settings-api.ts — provider_harnesses currently surfaced at :137,595,859-861,987-998) with a read-only built-in provider→harness default map computed from getBuiltInDefaultHarness for all 10 providers, so the frontend never duplicates the mapping in TSX. (2) In providerConfigForSave (settings-api.ts:731-739), add explicit delete handling: when the incoming provider harness value is absent or empty string, REMOVE the harness key from the provider's YAML node instead of merging/keeping it. Round-trip tests in the existing settings-api suite: save with override persists the key; save with '' removes the key from YAML; GET on unset returns no override plus the built-in map.

items.title: The settings GET response returns a built-in provider→harness defaults map covering all 10 providers, sourced from getBuiltInDefaultHarness
Saving a provider with an empty/absent harness value deletes the harness key from the YAML (round-trip test reads the file back)
Saving an explicit override persists and survives a GET round-trip unchanged
Settings → Providers: honest Default(<built-in>) harness selects

narrative.Action: Per PRD WI-7 frontend half in src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/Settings/SettingsPage.tsx: the per-provider harness selects (value computed at :1609 as `?? 'claude-code'`; selects at :1821-1832 and the openrouter card at :1905-1907) currently lie about the effective default. Change each select: first option value '' labeled `Default (<built-in label>)` (e.g. 'Default (Codex)' for openai) computed from the built-in map in the settings GET payload (never a local TSX map); `value={provider_harnesses[p] ?? ''}`; selecting '' deletes the override key (follow the Flywheel pattern — RolesPanel.tsx:517 `<option value="">` → patch with undefined). Apply identically to the openrouter select. Component tests: unset shows '', the default option label names the correct built-in harness per provider, choosing '' issues a save without the harness key.

items.title: With no override set, each provider's harness select renders value '' with first option labeled Default (<built-in>) — e.g. openai shows Default (Codex), kimi shows Default (Pi)
Selecting the Default option saves a payload without that provider's harness key (the override is deleted, not set to claude-code)
The openrouter card's harness select renders the same default-state behavior (Default label, '' value, key deletion on clear)
An explicitly chosen harness still persists and re-renders as that value after reload (round-trip via UI test or mocked API)
Settings → Roles: harness override select on every role card

narrative.Action: Per PRD WI-8 in src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/Settings/RolesPanel.tsx: the Flywheel card already has the exact control (verified ~:512-525 — select with `<option value="">Provider default</option>` + three explicit options, saving via `saveMutation.mutate({ role, patch: { harness: value || undefined } })`). Extract it into a local `RoleHarnessSelect` component and render it on EVERY role card (plan, work, strike, review, test, ship, flywheel) next to the existing per-role ModelPicker, with `aria-label="<Role> harness"`. Empty-option label: 'Provider default'. Backend persistence already works via saveRoleConfig — no server change. Tests follow the existing RolesPanel pattern: each role card exposes its labeled select; saving patches roles.<role>.harness; clearing patches it to undefined.

items.title: Every role card (plan, work, strike, review, test, ship, flywheel) renders a harness select with aria-label '<Role> harness' offering Provider default + claude-code + pi + codex
Choosing an explicit harness for a role saves a patch setting roles.<role>.harness to that value (test)
Choosing Provider default saves a patch with roles.<role>.harness undefined, clearing the override (test)
Shared branding registry: PROVIDER_BRANDS + HARNESS_BRANDS

narrative.Action: Per PRD WI-9: promote src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/chat/ProviderIcons.tsx into a new src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/shared/branding/ module exporting: `interface BrandDef { id: string; label: string; color: string; Icon: ComponentType<SVGProps<SVGSVGElement>> }`, `PROVIDER_BRANDS: Record<ProviderName, BrandDef>` (all 10 providers — initially the existing Anthropic/OpenAI inline SVGs from ProviderIcons.tsx:6-18 plus letter-badge-backed Icon stubs for the other 8 until the provider-marks bead replaces them), `HARNESS_BRANDS: Record<Harness, BrandDef>` (claude-code→Anthropic mark, codex→OpenAI mark, pi→styled π glyph in a brand-colored tile — pi-ai has no formal logo), and components `<ProviderLogo provider/>`, `<HarnessLogo harness/>`, `<ProviderDot/>`. Labels in BrandDef are the single source that will replace the duplicated PROVIDER_LABELS maps (RolesPanel.tsx:148, shared/ModelPicker/ModelPicker.tsx:59) in later beads. Migrate PROVIDER_COLORS values into BrandDef.color. ProviderLetterBadge survives only as the fallback for unknown ids. Keep chat/ProviderIcons.tsx compiling as a thin re-export so existing consumers don't break until their rollout beads land. SVGs use currentColor, matching the existing AnthropicIcon/OpenAIIcon pattern.

items.title: Given Record-typed exports, shared/branding stores 10 PROVIDER_BRANDS entries and 3 HARNESS_BRANDS entries each carrying id, label, color, and Icon; typecheck fails on a missing provider or harness
<HarnessLogo harness="pi"/> renders the π glyph tile; claude-code and codex render the Anthropic and OpenAI marks respectively (component test)
<ProviderLogo> renders the registry Icon for known providers and falls back to ProviderLetterBadge for unknown ids (component test)
Every existing consumer of chat/ProviderIcons.tsx still renders through the thin re-export (typecheck and the existing frontend tests run green)
Real SVG marks for the eight letter-badged providers
